title: Improved adeno-associated virus vectors
abstract: The present invention relates AAV genomes, vectors and nucleic acids containing variant capsid protein sequences that allow for increased titer, along with improved packing during recombinant AAV vector particle production. Titers improved several fold, without a similar increase in residual packaged hcDNA, along with maintaining percent full capsid ratio. In addition viral proteins expression and ratios remained unchanged with the variants.
claims:
Claims Claims What is claimed is: 1. An adeno-associated virus (AAV) genome comprising a variant membrane associated accessory protein (MAAP) nucleic acid sequence having a mutation selected from the group consisting of a stop codon substitution at a codon position corresponding to amino acid positions W19, C20, S27, S57 and S88, an amino acid substitution at position LI, an amino acid substitution at position C20, an amino acid substitution at position C21, and combinations thereof, wherein the codon positions correspond to the amino acid positions in consensus MAAP amino acid sequence SEQ ID NO:1. 2. The AAV genome of claim 1, wherein the genome comprises a substitution mutation in at least one of amino acid positions LI, C20 and C21 and a stop codon substitution at an amino acid position selected from the group consisting of W19, C20, S27, S57 and S88. 3. The AAV genome of any one of claims 1 to 2, wherein the genome comprises a substitution mutation at amino acid position C20 and a stop codon substitution at an amino acid position selected from the group consisting of W19, C20, S27, S57 and S88. 4. The AAV genome of any one of claims 1 to 2, wherein the genome comprises a substitution mutation at amino acid position C21 and a stop codon substitution at an amino acid position selected from the group consisting of W19, C20, S27, S57 and S88. 5. The AAV genome of any one of claims 1 to 2, wherein the genome comprises a substitution mutation at amino acid position LI and a stop codon substitution at a position selected from the group consisting of amino acid positions W19, C20, S27, S57 and S88. 6. The AAV genome of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the substitution mutation at amino acid position LI is an LIP substitution mutation. 7. The AAV genome of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the genome comprises a substitution mutation at one of amino acid positions C20 and C21 and a stop codon substitution at an amino acid position selected from the group consisting of S27, S57 and S88. 8. The AAV genome of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the genome comprises a substitution mutation at both of amino acid positions C20 and C21 and a stop codon substitution at an amino acid position selected from the group consisting of S27, S57 and S88. 9. The AAV genome of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the substitution mutation at amino acid position C20 is selected from the group consisting of C20S, C20Y and C20F substitution mutations. 10. The AAV genome of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the substitution mutation at amino acid position C21 is a C21S substitution mutation. 11. The AAV genome of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the stop codon substitution is at amino acid position W19. 12. The AAV genome of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the stop codon substitution is at amino acid position C20. 13. The AAV genome of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the stop codon substitution is at amino acid position S27. 14. The AAV genome of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the stop codon substitution is at amino acid position S57. 15. The AAV genome of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the stop codon substitution is at amino acid position S88. 16. The AAV genome of any one of claims 1 to 15, wherein the AAV genome is selected from the group consisting of a naturally-occurring serotype and a non-naturally-occurring serotype. 17. The AAV genome of claim 16, wherein the AAV genome is a non-naturally-occurring serotype. 18. The AAV genome of claim 16, wherein the genome is a naturally-occurring serotype. 19. The AAV genome of claim 18, wherein the AAV genome comprises a serotype 6 genome. 20. The AAV genome of claim 18, wherein the AAV genome comprises a serotype 2 genome. 21. The AAV genome of claim 18, wherein the AAV genome is selected from a serotype 1, serotype 3, serotype 4, serotype 5, serotype 7, serotype 8, serotype 9, serotype 10, serotype 11, serotype 12, serotype 13, serotype rh8 and serotype rhlO genome. 22. The AAV genome of any one of claims 1 to 21, wherein the AAV genome comprises a VP1 nucleic sequence, and wherein the MAAP and VP1 nucleotide sequences encode MAAP and VP1 peptide sequences that each have at least 80% homology to wild type, with the proviso that the MAAP nucleotide sequence consists of at least one mutation as defined in any one of claims 1 to 9. 23. The AAV genome of claim 22, wherein the MAAP and VP1 peptide sequences each have at least 90% homology to wild type. 24. The AAV genome of claim 22, wherein the MAAP and VP1 peptide sequences each have at least 95% homology to wild type. 25. A producer cell that produces adeno-associated virus, the producer cell comprising the adeno-associated virus genome of any one of claims 1 to 24. 26. The producer cell of claim 25, wherein the producer cell is eukaryotic. 27. The producer cell of claim 25, wherein the producer cell is mammalian. 28. The producer cell of claim 25, wherein the producer cell is a human cell. 29. The producer cell of claim 26, wherein the producer cell is selected from of yeast cells and insect cells. 30. A method for producing adeno-associated virus, the method comprising: introducing the AAV genome of any of claims 1 to 24 into a cell to create a producer cell and then culturing the producer cell whereby said producer cell produces adeno-associated virus. 31. The method of claim 30, further comprising harvesting said adeno-associated virus, where said harvested adeno-associated virus comprises a transgene. 32. The method of any one of claims 30 to 31 , wherein the titer of the produced adeno- associated virus is greater than the titer achieved with an adeno-associated virus genome comprising a corresponding wild-type MAAP sequence. 33. Adeno-associated virus produced by the process of any of claims 30 to 32. 34. A vector comprising a variant AAV capsid gene sequence, the variant AAV capsid gene sequence comprising a mutated membrane associated accessory protein (MAAP) nucleic acid sequence having a mutation selected from the group consisting of a stop codon substitution at a codon position corresponding to amino acid positions W19, C20, S27, S57 and S88, an amino acid substitution at amino acid position LI, an amino acid substitution at amino acid position C20, and an amino acid substitution at amino acid position C21, and combinations thereof, 

wherein the codon positions align with the amino acid positions in consensus MAAP amino acid sequence SEQ ID NO: 1. 35. The vector of claim 34, wherein the variant AAV capsid gene sequence comprises a substitution mutation of at least one of amino acid positions LI, C20 and C21 and a stop codon substitution at an amino acid position selected from the group consisting of W19, C20, S27, S57 and S88. 36. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 35, wherein the variant AAV capsid gene sequence comprises a substitution mutation at amino acid position C20 and a stop codon substitution at an amino acid position selected from the group consisting of W19, C20, S27, S57 and S88. 37. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 35, wherein the variant AAV capsid gene sequence comprises a substitution mutation at amino acid position C21 and a stop codon substitution at an amino acid position selected from the group consisting of W19, C20, S27, S57 and S88. 38. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 35, wherein the variant AAV capsid gene sequence comprises a substitution mutation at amino acid position LI and a stop codon substitution at an amino acid position selected from the group consisting of W19, C20, S27, S57 and S88. 39. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 29, wherein the substitution mutation at amino acid position LI is an LIP substitution mutation. 40. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 39, wherein the variant AAV capsid gene sequence comprises a substitution mutation at one of amino acid positions C20 and C21 and a stop codon substitution at an amino acid position selected from the group consisting of S27, S57 and S88. 41. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 39, wherein the variant AAV capsid gene sequence comprises a substitution mutation at both of amino acid positions C20 and C21 and a stop codon substitution at an amino acid position selected from the group consisting of S27, S57 and S88. 42. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 41 , wherein the substitution mutation at amino acid position C20 is selected from the group consisting of C20S, C20Y and C20F substitution mutations. 43. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 41, wherein the substitution mutation at amino acid position C21 is a C21S substitution mutation. 44. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 43, wherein the stop codon substitution is at amino acid position W19. 45. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 43, wherein the stop codon substitution is at amino acid position C20. 46. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 43, wherein the stop codon substitution is at amino acid position S27. 47. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 43, wherein the stop codon substitution is at amino acid position S57. 48. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 43, wherein the stop codon substitution is at amino acid position S88. 49. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 48, where the variant capsid gene sequence is selected from the group consisting of a naturally-occurring serotype and a non-naturally- occurring serotype. 50. The vector of claim 49, wherein the variant capsid gene sequence is a non-naturally- occurring serotype. 51. The vector of claim 49, wherein the variant capsid gene sequence is a naturally-occurring serotype. 52. The vector of claim 51 , wherein the variant capsid gene sequence is a serotype 6 capsid gene sequence. 53. The vector of claim 51, wherein the variant capsid gene sequence is a serotype 2 capsid gene sequence. 54. The vector of claim 51, wherein the variant capsid gene sequence is selected from a serotype 1, serotype 3, serotype 4, serotype 5, serotype 7, serotype 8, serotype 9, serotype 10, serotype 11, serotype 12, serotype 13, serotype rh8 and serotype rhlO capsid gene sequences. 55. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 54, wherein the vector comprises a VP1 nucleic sequence, and wherein the MAAP and VP1 nucleotide sequences encode MAAP and VP1 peptide sequences that each have at least 80% homology to wild type, with the proviso that the modified capsid nucleotide sequence consists of at least one mutation as defined in any one of claims 28 to 36. 56. The vector of claim 55, wherein the MAAP and VP1 peptide sequences each have at least 90% homology to wild type. 57. The vector of claim 55, wherein the MAAP and VP1 peptide sequences each have at least 95% homology to wild type. 58. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 57, wherein the vector further comprises a nucleic acid sequence encoding a rep gene. 59. The vector of any one of claims 34 to 58, wherein the vector is a plasmid vector. 60. A vector system comprising the vector of any one of claims 34 to 59 and a second vector comprising a transgene flanked by ITR sequences. 61. The vector system of claim 60, further comprising a third vector comprising adenovirus helper sequences. 62. The vector system of any one of claims 60 to 51, wherein the second and third vectors are plasmid vectors. 63. A producer cell line comprising the vector of any one of claims 34 to 59 or vector system of any one of claims 60 to 62. 64. The producer cell of claim 63, wherein the producer cell is eukaryotic. 65. The producer cell of claim 63, wherein the producer cell is mammalian. 66. The producer cell of claim 65, wherein the producer cell is a human cell. 67. The producer cell of claim 63, wherein the producer cell is selected from of yeast cells and insect cells. 68. A method of for producing adeno-associated virus, the method comprising: introducing the vector or vector system of any of claims 34 to 62 into a cell to create a producer cell and then culturing the producer cell whereby the producer cell produces adeno-associated virus. 69. The method of claim 68, wherein the titer of the produced adeno-associated virus is greater than the titer achieved with an adeno-associated virus genome comprising a corresponding wild-type MAAP sequence.
